title: If VCCR drops 15% with no release — a forensic, not a panic
subtitle: Scenario: 3 weeks post-launch VCCR falls 92% → 77%. No release, campaign, or outage.
diagram_alt: Four-branch hypothesis tree: UX regression, cohort shift, backend data drift, external trigger.
analysis_plan: Segment drop by platform / app version / OS → isolate iOS-only vs version bug
Funnel drop-off by consent category → marketing-only (JIT) vs uniform (cohort/external)
Ledger event volume vs app analytics volume → backend data loss
Correlate with acquisition source / campaign ID → cohort shift
Session recordings at consent screens → rage-click / UX confusion
root_cause: Five-Whys + cohort isolation. Example: opt-in fell → JIT fires at checkout → address event mis-mapped to checkout in v8.4.2 → release regression in trigger logic.
next_steps: 0-24h: freeze rollout if <80%, verify pipeline, alert legal
1-7d: A/B JIT timing, hotfix regression, in-app survey
7-30d: re-engage incomplete consent, fix micro-copy, transparency post
notes: The hypothesis tree prevents panic; the analysis plan is a forensic checklist. Key: is it a UX bug, a backend leak, or a market shift?
